headline.text: L1 MM4 *shut down at 09:05* for silver visibility — L3 TG4 unstable since midnight — but most issues cleared including two L1 caramel outages and L3 bolt failure.

per_plant_summary.L1: S3 ran out of caramel twice overnight (20:59 and 00:29), both cleared and line restarted. S2 slowed to 1.1 at 20:12 on cold corn syrup, resolved by running dual kettles and back to 1.4 at 21:06. X-ray test card failures on MM3 and MM5 at 18:20 were traced to bad cards, not machines — 4 pallets reworked successfully. MM4 shut down at 09:05 for excessive silver visibility and remains down.
per_plant_summary.L2: ML3 launched caramel cups successfully — client approved product at 10:04, line running 10 mpm. ML2 had two cooler mold jams from bent rail #75, both cleared but rail needs replacement during next clean. FFS ran out of WIP 21101 at 07:02 and stopped, waiting on transfer from L3. Grinding found blue glove foreign material in Brookies at 00:16, pallet on hold.
per_plant_summary.L3: ML1 C6 roller bolt broke at 15:15, metal debris cleaned and sanitized, line back at 16:35. ML4 running 2 of 4 TGs — TG4 keeps going down on chocolate blow-off since 00:49, TG5 lot code issue fixed at 00:48. Crew managing Gondola manually to reduce scrap (~1,000 lbs total). Bar quality improved through shift after bottom bubble adjustments. Countdown board had 47k-piece error at 08:49, corrected.

resolved.title: S2 C1 conveyor belt stopped
resolved.summary: C1 belt stopped at 12:04, operations paused; broken cable wasn't making contact.
resolved.resolution: Maintenance fixed broken cable at 12:21 — "already fixed the problem C1, A cable was broken" (Victor Romero Sr.).

resolved.title: S2 cold corn syrup slowing production
resolved.summary: All corn syrup totes cold despite being plugged in; line at risk of slowing or stopping.
resolved.resolution: Slowed to 1.1 at 20:12, ran both kitchen sides A and B to maintain caramel, back to 1.4 at 21:06 — "going to increase it to 1.4 now" (Elizabeth Hernandez).

resolved.title: S3 X-ray failures on MM3 and MM5
resolved.summary: MM3 failed to reject stainless steel 2.0 test card at 18:20; MM5 failed non-ferrous 2mm card at 18:57.
resolved.resolution: Root cause was bad test card, not x-rays; new cards passed all three machines. Four pallets (MM3: 18:20–19:00, MM5: 18:50–19:10) reworked successfully by 19:42 — "Pallets reworked successfully" (Victor Mejia).

resolved.title: S3 ran out of caramel, line stopped at 20:59
resolved.summary: Reserve kettle empty, depositor out of caramel at 20:59 on 5/27.
resolved.resolution: Line resumed at 21:23 — "Line was paused from 9:01pm–9:23pm" (William Beattie).

resolved.title: S3 ran out of caramel again at 00:29
resolved.summary: Depositor ran out of caramel at 00:29; caustic clean started at 01:04.
resolved.resolution: Heavy wash, reheat, and cleaning complete; line restarted at 04:01 — "Line already started" (Luis Falcon).

resolved.title: S3 metal detector belt stopped at 09:20
resolved.summary: MD belt suddenly stopped, unable to restart even after reset; line paused.
resolved.resolution: Two hard resets (30 and 40 seconds) brought it back; QA cards passed, line running at 09:24 — "The line is back up and running" (S3 Enrober).

resolved.title: S4 high APC results requiring level 1 clean
resolved.summary: Post-3rd-clean APC sponge samples came back high; conducted level 1 clean on all belts including T7-1, T9-1, and guillotine belt.
resolved.resolution: ATP tests passed satisfactorily at 22:35 — "all belts on the line, and they passed satisfactorily" (Alfonsina Gil).

resolved.title: S4 caramel spec missing piece dimensions
resolved.summary: Theegarten caramel spec showed only 12g weight target with no height, width, or length — line couldn't proceed without clarity.
resolved.resolution: Agreed at 08:35 to run at current piece size and update specs to match Theegarten output — "samples will be kept and reviewed to determine those specs" (Miriah Manzanares).

resolved.title: S3 cardboard under white paper at MM4 and MM5
resolved.summary: Cardboard placed underneath matrix paper, not allowed by quality; unclear who placed it.
resolved.resolution: Removed and replaced with fresh paper at 08:35 — "She removed it and replaced the paper" (Mariela Gudino).

resolved.title: ML3 new caramel cups product launch
resolved.summary: Launch day for new caramel cup product with client on-site; recipe adjustments needed for deposit quality.
resolved.resolution: Client approved product at 10:04; running at 10 mpm with good weight and appearance — "Client is happy. And best thing, we are running at 10mpm" (Erandy Garcilazo Ceja).

resolved.title: ML3 printer not working at start of shift
resolved.summary: Printer down, delaying BBD placement verification and production start.
resolved.resolution: Maintenance fixed printer; QA confirmed BBD placement correct at 06:03 — "Printer is good and the BBD place is set" (Gustavo Rodas).

resolved.title: ML2 mold jam in cooler at 05:52
resolved.summary: Mold jam stopped line at 05:52; maintenance called.
resolved.resolution: Bent rail caused jam; line back up at 06:54 — "Bent rail in the cooler caused the jam" (Dave Ruff).

resolved.title: ML2 second mold jam in cooler at 07:42
resolved.summary: Another mold jam in cooler; maintenance notified.
resolved.resolution: Line back up at 08:03 — "Line is back up" (Alex Mendoza).

resolved.title: BEQ FW wrap material shortage
resolved.summary: Only 4 of 5 cases of flow wrap arrived with overnight shipment; additional wrap needed for production.
resolved.resolution: Team truck arrived at 05:30 as scheduled; wrap received into RF Smart by 06:42 — "BEQ FW has arrived & will be received" (Joey Michael Dudney).

resolved.title: ML3 temper meter probe damaged
resolved.summary: Probe broke earlier in the week; maintenance temporarily repaired it.
resolved.resolution: Raymond Fleming has new probe; maintenance got it back together — "Maintenance was able to get it back together" (Alan Todd at 23:10).

resolved.title: ML1 C6 roller bolt broke at 15:15
resolved.summary: Screw rubbing roller guard caused squeaky noise; bolt broke, creating metal particles and dust in product zone.
resolved.resolution: Area cleaned and sanitized by 16:07, QA inspection passed, line running at 16:35 — "Line is running" (Nathan Gevenois).

resolved.title: ML1 Cavanna infeed sensor causing jams
resolved.summary: Frequent jams on Cavanna infeed at 19:06; cleaned rails but issue persisted.
resolved.resolution: Sensor not reading correctly; fixed and running better by 19:06 — "the issue ended up being the sensor not reading correctly it's been fixed" (Nathan Gevenois).

resolved.title: ML1 C6 belt tracking off at 23:18
resolved.summary: C6 belt moving to south side; maintenance working on alignment.
resolved.resolution: Corrected by maintenance — belt running correctly by 00:26 (next check-in shows "running correctly").

resolved.title: ML4 TG5 lot code time stuck at midnight
resolved.summary: TG5 lot code time set to midnight and won't change; maintenance looking for password.
resolved.resolution: Fixed at 00:48 and working properly — "TG5 lot code time was fixed and is working properly" (Frederick Peairs).

resolved.title: ML1 shakers 3 and 4 alarm at 08:27
resolved.summary: Alarm triggered on shakers 3 and 4; maintenance called.
resolved.resolution: Motors tripped, VFD power cycled; no physical adjustments needed — cleared by 08:32 (Nathan Ruiz).

resolved.title: ML1 countdown board error
resolved.summary: Countdown numbers didn't match NetSuite; extra 47,127 pieces added that weren't produced.
resolved.resolution: Reviewed with Fredy Montalvan, corrected at 08:49 — "The countdown board has now been corrected" (Grey Boscan).
